report.answer: Positive feedback appears on Perceived value, Lens tinting perception, and Comfort perception. Lens tinting perception is partially supported by product images, while Perceived value and Comfort perception are mainly review-supported and lack direct product-page substantiation. The most important negative issue is Rubber insert durability failure.

strengths.label: Perceived value
strengths.summary: Positive feedback on Perceived value comes from user reviews, but no direct product-page text or reliable image evidence was retrieved.

strengths.label: Lens tinting perception
strengths.summary: One reviewer affirms 'excellent tinting', and product images show visible lens coloration under standard lighting — providing weak but observable visual support for tint presence (though exact hue remains unspecified).

strengths.label: Comfort perception
strengths.summary: Positive feedback on Comfort perception comes from user reviews, but no direct product-page text or reliable image evidence was retrieved.

weaknesses.label: Rubber insert durability failure
weaknesses.summary: One user reports that the rubber insert near the ear deteriorated and fell apart in less than two years.

weaknesses.label: Potential optical magnification affecting depth perception
weaknesses.summary: One user reports that the lenses slightly magnify vision, making the ground appear closer during walking or outdoor navigation.

evidence_gaps.label: Missing rubber insert durability disclosure
evidence_gaps.summary: No product-page warranty, material composition, or durability testing detail was retrieved for the rubber insert near the ear.

evidence_gaps.label: Missing optical distortion validation
evidence_gaps.summary: No product-page evidence was retrieved for optical neutrality, distortion control, or magnification-related validation.

evidence_gaps.label: Missing explicit lens tint hue disclosure
evidence_gaps.summary: Product images visually show tinted lenses, but the product text does not explicitly name the tint color or hue.

evidence_gaps.label: Price substantiation gap
evidence_gaps.summary: The product is positively perceived as having an excellent price, but the listed price and comparative pricing context were not retrieved.

evidence_gaps.label: Comfort substantiation gap
evidence_gaps.summary: Comfort is positively mentioned by a user review, but no product-page text or reliable image evidence was retrieved to substantiate the claim.

suggestions.suggestion: Add durability-validation notes and warranty scope for the rubber ear insert, and describe the actual material and test scope without claiming unsupported lifetime thresholds.
reason: The rubber insert near the ear is cited as failing within two years, yet no warranty, material specification, or durability claim appears anywhere in text or image content — creating unmanaged customer expectations.
Retrievals confirm zero mention of rubber composition, reinforcement, or warranty in all examined text blocks and image regions.
suggestions.replay_note: Replay continuity: the issue around Missing rubber insert durability disclosure also appeared in the previous run.

suggestions.suggestion: Add product-page optical verification notes and publish the measured distortion or prism-control results before making strong visual-performance claims.
reason: No product-page claims or test data about optical clarity, distortion control, non-prescription status, or measured visual performance were retrieved.
No retrieved text or image content references optical clarity, edge distortion, or real-world visual use cases.
suggestions.replay_note: Replay continuity: the issue around Missing optical distortion validation also appeared in the previous run.

suggestions.suggestion: State the current selling price and any active discount or list-price reference directly in the primary product metadata so the value claim can be checked quickly.
reason: Price is praised as 'excellent' but no numeric price or comparative context (e.g., original vs. current, % off) appears in any retrieved text block — undermining credibility of the value claim.
All retrievals for price-related queries returned only category breadcrumbs or product name, with no pricing data found.
suggestions.replay_note: Replay continuity: the issue around Price substantiation gap also appeared in the previous run.

suggestions.suggestion: State the exact lens tint in the primary product copy and include a color-accurate front-facing lens image so the tint claim can be checked visually.
reason: Product images visually show tinted lenses, but the product text does not explicitly name the tint color or hue.
suggestions.replay_note: Replay continuity: the issue around Missing explicit lens tint hue disclosure also appeared in the previous run.

suggestions.suggestion: Clarify the fit and comfort-related design cues on the product page, and add close-up visuals of the features that help users judge wear comfort.
reason: Comfort is positively mentioned by a user review, but no product-page text or reliable image evidence was retrieved to substantiate the claim.
suggestions.replay_note: Replay continuity: the issue around Comfort substantiation gap also appeared in the previous run.
